name: Fetchium
description: Token-efficient web search, extraction, and AI research as an MCP server. 12 tools: multi-backend search (15+ engines), query-aware content extraction, multi-source research with citations, YouTube analysis, Reddit/HN search. Rust-native, zero-install via npx.

tools.name: search
tools.description: Search the web with multi-backend support (Google, DuckDuckGo, Brave, Bing, Tavily, Serper, Exa, and more). Returns ranked, deduplicated results with scores.

tools.name: fetch
tools.description: Fetch and extract clean content from any URL using the 5-layer CEP cascade: CSS selectors → readability → headless JS → PDF → OCR.

tools.name: summarize
tools.description: Summarize content from a URL or text in a token-efficient format with optional AI synthesis.

tools.name: research
tools.description: Multi-source research pipeline: search, fetch top sources, extract, rank, and synthesize a cited report.

tools.name: compare
tools.description: Compare multiple products, topics, or items across sources with evidence-backed analysis.

tools.name: extract
tools.description: Extract structured data (facts, tables, lists) from a URL using query-aware QATBE extraction.

tools.name: youtube
tools.description: Analyze YouTube videos: get transcripts, summaries, chapter breakdowns, and key insights.

tools.name: reddit
tools.description: Search Reddit for community discussions, opinions, and recommendations on any topic.

tools.name: news
tools.description: Get recent news and current events from multiple news sources with temporal ranking.

tools.name: academic
tools.description: Search academic papers, research, and scientific literature from multiple scholarly sources.

tools.name: validate
tools.description: Validate claims and facts by cross-referencing multiple sources with confidence scoring.

tools.name: doctor
tools.description: Run a health check on the Fetchium server, verifying backend connectivity and configuration.
